question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call request that triggers many short Hibernate SQL queries in rapid succession, each resetting the elapsed time check in statementInspector() to a value well below requestTimeout, effectively allowing the total request processing time to far exceed requestTimeout and holding server threads causing a non-network-based DoS?
